# zerp consumer instructions for LLMs

`@emirotin/zerp` is a zero-config presentation framework. Slides are authored in HTML, Markdown, or a mix. The deck source of truth is `slides/`.

## Deck contract

- Put slides in `slides/**/*.html` and/or `slides/**/*.md`; files are ordered lexicographically (`00-`, `10-`, `20-` prefixes).
- Assets live under `slides/` too; use relative paths (`./images/foo.jpg`) ‚Äî they are rewritten at build time.
- Each `.html` file contains one or more `<div class="slide">` blocks. Each `.md` file is auto-wrapped; separate multiple slides with `---` on its own line.
- Never hand-edit the generated `index.html`.

## Slide numbering

**"Slide N" always means the 1-based deck position** ‚Äî the number the runtime counter shows to presenter and audience. File name prefixes only order files; a file may contain several slides, so a prefix is NOT a slide number. The runtime counter, the URL hash (`#N`), `zerp check` findings, and `zerp slides` all use deck positions.

- Before editing "slide N", run `zerp slides .` (add `--json` for tooling): it prints each slide's position, source file, in-file ordinal, and title. Confirm the target by its heading.
- Every composed slide div carries source-tracing attributes: `data-zerp-src` (source file), `data-zerp-src-slide` (`i/n` within that file), `data-zerp-index` (deck position).
- In a running deck, press `s` to toggle a source badge showing the active slide's position and source file.

## Design system ‚Äî colors

Colors come from CSS custom properties (design tokens). The framework ships a dark and a light theme; tokens flip automatically. **Never hardcode hex/rgb colors. Never invent new colors. Pick tokens by meaning.**

Neutrals:

- `--zerp-bg` page background ¬∑ `--zerp-surface` cards/panels ¬∑ `--zerp-border` borders
- `--zerp-text` body text ¬∑ `--zerp-muted` secondary text ‚Äî use at body size (‚â•1.25em/20px), or at smaller sizes only with weight 700
- `--zerp-faint` decorative glyphs ONLY (arrows, dividers) ‚Äî **never use for text**

Accent hues (each in 4 roles): `blue green orange purple red amber teal`

- `--zerp-<hue>` ‚Äî colored text on the page/surface (use at body size or larger)
- `--zerp-<hue>-solid` + `--zerp-on-solid` ‚Äî filled backgrounds (badges, fills) and text on them
- `--zerp-<hue>-tint` + `--zerp-<hue>-on-tint` ‚Äî subtle washes (highlight cards/rows) and text on them

Semantic aliases: `--zerp-accent` (blue), `--zerp-ok` (green), `--zerp-warn` (amber), `--zerp-danger` (red). Prefer semantic names when the color carries meaning; hue names for categorical coding.

## Typography rules

- Body text is 1.25em (~20px). **Never set text below 1em; `.sm` (0.9√ó) is the only sanctioned shrink.** Prefer trimming content over shrinking text.
- `.sm` is for inline spans within body text; on a bare paragraph it lands below the 16px floor and `zerp check` will flag it.
- Do not combine `.sm` with `.muted` ‚Äî small muted text fails contrast.
- For a detail line under a heading or bold statement use `.sub` (1em, bold, muted ‚Äî safe by construction). This is the idiom for the second line of a `.key-thought` and for card sub-text.
- Colored text classes are bold by design (emphasis). Do not fight the weight.
- Decorative glyphs (arrows, ‚ñ≤/‚ñº markers) may be small: mark them `aria-hidden="true"` ‚Äî screen readers and `zerp check` skip them. Keep the real information in text.
- Captions: use `.caption` or `<figcaption>` ‚Äî already sized and colored safely.
- Direct slide children space themselves automatically (14px rhythm). Do not add margins for ordinary spacing; use an inline margin only to deliberately tighten or widen one specific gap. For a width-capped centered block use `.narrow` (or `margin-inline: auto`; a full `margin: 0 auto` kills the automatic rhythm).
- Utility classes reliably override component defaults (component "soft defaults" are declared with `:where()`): `h3.accent` recolors a heading, `.value.red` recolors a stat, `p.xl` enlarges a paragraph, `.year.xl` enlarges a timeline year. Say what you mean with classes instead of inline styles.

## Built-in classes

Emphasis: `.accent .ok .warn .danger .blue .green .orange .purple .red .amber .teal` (bold colored text) ¬∑ `.muted` ¬∑ `.mono` ¬∑ `.lg .xl .sm .sub` ¬∑ `.raised` (elevation shadow for images/cards)
Layout: `.cols-2 .cols-3 .cols-4` (equal grids) ¬∑ `.row` (centered flex row) ¬∑ `.stack` (column) ¬∑ `.spread` (space-between) ¬∑ `.center` (center text ‚Äî safe on headings, paragraphs, and containers) ¬∑ `.narrow` (width-capped centered block) ¬∑ `.grow` ¬∑ `.slide.top` (top-align a busy slide)
Components:

- `.card` ‚Äî surface box; add `.tint-<hue>` for a colored wash, `.edge-<hue>` (or `.edge-ok/.edge-warn/.edge-danger/.edge-accent`) to recolor the border by meaning ‚Äî works on `.key-thought`, `.timeline .item`, and `.steps` cells too
- `.stat` (`.value` + `.label` children) inside `.stat-row` ‚Äî big numbers; color a value with a hue class (`.value.red`); for stat√óoperator compositions (`a √ó b = c`) use `.row` instead so operators center vertically
- `.concept` (`.icon` + `.label` children) ‚Äî definition-slide header: big emoji icon over a shouty accent label; follow with a plain or `.muted` tagline paragraph
- `.slide.title` ‚Äî title-slide recipe: `h1` + `h3` (auto-accent) + `.meta` block for presenter/date
- `.compare` ‚Äî two children side by side with a "vs" divider (`data-vs="‚Üí"` to customize)
- `.flow` ‚Äî process row, arrows auto-inserted between children (works for text spans and for `.card` children)
- `.steps` ‚Äî auto-numbered card grid (children: plain divs with `h3` + `p`)
- `.timeline` with `.item` (`.year`, `.label`) ‚Äî milestones
- `.key-thought` ‚Äî boxed takeaway (one per slide max)
- `.pill` ‚Äî inline badge; combine with `.ok/.warn/.danger/.accent` or `.tint-<hue>`
- `.interactive-badge` ‚Äî marks slides that react to ‚Üì/‚Üë ¬∑ `.step-hint` ‚Äî the "‚Üì do X" affordance line for stepped slides
- `.block-label` ‚Äî small top-left section label
- `.img-row` ‚Äî row of images; wrap each img in a `figure` + `figcaption` for captioned entries ¬∑ `figure` + `figcaption` for single captioned images ¬∑ `figure.corner` ‚Äî pin a small reference figure (poster, book cover) to the slide's top-right, out of the content flow
- `table` is styled by default (add `.mono` for numeric tables, `.compact` for dense centered data grids ‚Äî combine as `class="mono compact"`; `.tint-green`/`.tint-red` on `<tr>` to highlight rows); side-by-side tables go in a `.row`, which lays them out by its gap
- `blockquote`, `ol` (numbered takeaways), `ul` (arrow bullets), `code`, `pre`, `kbd` are styled by default

## Slide recipes

Title: `# Big title` + `### subtitle` (Markdown). Section divider: `.block-label` + `h1` + `h3`. Bullets: `##` + `-` list. Two-column: `<div class="cols-2">` with text and image/card. Big numbers: `.stat-row` of `.stat`s. Comparison: `.compare` of two `.card`s. Process: `.flow` of spans. Steps: `.steps` of divs. History: `.timeline`. Quote: Markdown `>`. Takeaways: Markdown ordered list. Data: Markdown table. Closing: `# Thanks` + contact in `.muted`.

Prefer Markdown for text-first slides; HTML for layout-heavy or interactive slides.

## Theming

- Decks are theme-neutral: tokens adapt to dark/light automatically. The presenter picks the default via `zerp build --theme dark|light|system` (default: `system`); viewers can override with the ‚óê switch or the `t` key (persisted in localStorage).
- Because both themes ship, anything you author must work in both ‚Äî that is why hardcoded colors are forbidden.

## Validation loop (required)

After authoring or editing slides, ALWAYS run:

```bash
zerp check .
```

Fix every `‚úó` error and `‚öÝ` warning (font too small, text contrast too low, or a surface whose background blends into what is behind it ‚Äî the report names the slide file, the element, and suggests fixes). Re-run until clean. Items marked `?` (backgrounds with images/gradients) need a manual look. `zerp build` prints the same summary.

`zerp serve` live-reloads the browser on any change under `slides/` ‚Äî the page returns to the same slide (URL hash) and replays the active slide's steps. Built decks contain no reload client.

## Interaction model

`zerp` handles navigation (arrows, space, PageUp/Down, Home/End). Down/Up arrows advance the current slide's step counter and dispatch `slide-next` / `slide-prev` events to it.

### Declarative step reveals (default ‚Äî no JavaScript)

Mark elements with step attributes; the runtime toggles classes as ‚Üì/‚Üë move the counter:

- `data-step="N"` ‚Äî appears once the counter reaches N (gets `.revealed`)
- `data-until-step="N"` ‚Äî disappears at N (gets `.step-done`)
- Space is reserved (visibility, not display), so stepping never reflows a centered slide.
- Add `.swap` to exchange same-sized content in place (a label, a glyph): swapped elements collapse instead of reserving, so the counterpart takes the slot.
- Highlight-type steps (element always visible, restyled at step N): keep `data-step` and override in deck CSS ‚Äî `.my-scope [data-step] { visibility: visible; }` plus `.my-scope [data-step].revealed { /* highlight */ }`.
- Glyph markers accompanying a highlight (‚ñ≤ ‚ñº next to a number): absolutely position them so they never skew centered content, and show them via the parent's `.revealed` (`.my-scope tr.revealed .marker { visibility: visible }`) ‚Äî no `data-step` needed on the marker itself.

```html
<div class="slide">
  <div class="interactive-badge">Interactive</div>
  <h2>Example</h2>
  <p>Always visible.</p>
  <p data-step="1">Appears on the first ‚Üì.</p>
  <div class="key-thought" data-step="2"><p>The payoff.</p></div>
  <p class="step-hint" data-until-step="2">‚Üì next</p>
</div>
```

### Scripted slides (escape hatch for real simulations)

For state that isn't a fixed reveal sequence (random draws, live counters), use a script (works in `.html` files and raw HTML inside `.md`):

```html
<div class="slide">
  <div class="interactive-badge">Interactive</div>
  <h2>Example</h2>
  <div id="demo-output"></div>
  <script>
    (function () {
      var slide = document.currentScript.closest(".slide");
      var step = 0;
      function render() {
        slide.querySelector("#demo-output").textContent = "Step " + step;
      }
      slide.addEventListener("slide-next", function () {
        if (step < 3) {
          step++;
          render();
        }
      });
      slide.addEventListener("slide-prev", function () {
        if (step > 0) {
          step--;
          render();
        }
      });
      render();
    })();
  </script>
</div>
```

Rules: scope queries to `document.currentScript.closest(".slide")`; keep state in the closure; make `slide-prev` reverse state; no global key listeners; no external dependencies. Build static markup in HTML and have the script toggle classes / set `textContent` ‚Äî do not assemble HTML strings with inline styles (static markup is what `zerp check` can verify). When a script must set a color, use a class or a token: `el.className = "value green"`.

## Custom CSS policy (last resort)

1. Reach for built-in classes and styled elements first ‚Äî they cover title, list, column, stat, comparison, flow, step, timeline, quote, table, and image slides.
2. If a layout truly needs custom CSS, put a `<style>` inside the slide file, use ONLY `var(--zerp-*)` tokens, keep text at 1em+.
3. A `<style>` block is document-global. For deck-wide styles the convention is a style-only file sorted first (e.g. `slides/00-styles.html` containing just a `<style>` ‚Äî it contributes no slide).
4. Re-run `zerp check` ‚Äî custom CSS is checked too (`:where()` is understood). Selectors it cannot verify are listed as skipped; keep them simple (tag/class selectors).

## What to avoid

- No full HTML documents inside slide files; no duplicated framework CSS or runtime.
- No hardcoded colors anywhere (including inline styles and scripts). Sole exemption: colors of depicted real-world objects (a playing card's white face, a roulette pocket's black) may stay literal ‚Äî they represent the object and must not flip with the theme.
- No text below 1em; no `--zerp-faint` for text; no `.sm .muted` combos.
- No `<div class="slide">` wrappers inside Markdown files (added automatically).
- Do not recreate navigation; do not edit generated `index.html`.

## Validation commands

Inside the zerp repo: `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint && pnpm format:check`.
In a consumer deck: `zerp check .` then `zerp serve .` / `zerp build .`.
